macro_events.event_summary: Fed Chair Powell announces he will remain on the Fed Board of Governors after his chair term ends, signaling continuity in monetary policy stance.
primary_drivers: Reduced uncertainty about Fed policy continuity / dovish-hawkish regime shift
Lower probability of a more dovish Trump-aligned chair packing the FOMC near-term
Marginal support for USD and real yields vs. expectations of a faster pivot

beneficiaries.rationale: Large money-center bank benefits from continued steady-rate regime and a credible Fed; Powell staying reduces tail risk of politicization that would steepen the curve via term premium but hurt bank credibility/credit spreads. Modest positive.

headlines: Kalshi bettors called it: Powell says he will stay on as Fed governor after chair term ends

macro_events.event_summary: CNBC reports a major dissent at the Fed (implying internal disagreement on rate path), Powell staying in his role, and Boeing taking what the article frames as an unwarranted stock hit.
primary_drivers: Fed policy uncertainty / dovish dissent signal raising odds of cuts
Powell continuity removes leadership-risk premium from rates/USD
Boeing-specific headline weakness creating potential dislocation in aerospace supply chain

beneficiaries.rationale: CNBC explicitly frames the Boeing decline as 'unwarranted', which is a direct buy-the-dip call from a high-visibility outlet (Cramer/Investing Club). Headline-driven mean reversion candidate over intraday/days.

GE Aerospace supplies LEAP engines for the 737 MAX; correlated rebound trade if Boeing dislocation is faded, plus benefits from any dovish Fed dissent via lower discount rate on long-duration aerospace cash flows.

losers.rationale: AAR Corp is an aftermarket/MRO play; if Boeing OEM narrative dominates and aerospace sentiment is mixed, less direct beneficiary of a BA-specific rebound.

headlines: Major Fed rate dissent, Powell to stay on — plus, Boeing takes an unwarranted hit
